title: Laocoon Statue Group
creator: Athanadoros; Hagesander; Polydoros
model_creator: Bernd Breuckmann; Mohamed Abdelaziz; Kacie Alaga; Chad Keller; David Koller; Lorenz Wurmser
description: Marble sculpture depicting the myth of Laocoon, a Trojan priest of Apollo. As divine punishment for warning the Trojans about accepting the Greeks' wooden horse, he and his sons were killed by flesh-eating snakes.
rights: Copyright 2010- Vatican Museums. Permission for use in this collection given by the Virtual World Heritage Lab at Indiana University (IU) Bloomington. This collection is an academic project within IU.
image_alt_text: Marble sculpture depicting the myth of Laocoon, a Trojan priest of Apollo. The sculpture shows Laocoon, a mature, bearded man, in between his two young sons as the three of them struggle against two large snakes.
